---
title: "Experiment 2"
output: html_document
---


```{r}
# Packages
library(lme4)
library(stats)
library(purrr)
library(lmerTest)  # optional, for p-values in summaries

df_allsubjs_trials <- read.csv(file.path("data","exp2_data.csv"))
# Remove Train Block 1 because it has hints
df_allsubjs_trials <- df_allsubjs_trials[df_allsubjs_trials$state_group>=0,] 

# Only analyze trials where participants deliberately submitted a solution (as opposed to time-outs)
df_allsubjs_trials <- df_allsubjs_trials[df_allsubjs_trials$"time_exceeded" < 1, ]

df_allsubjs_testtrials <- df_allsubjs_trials[df_allsubjs_trials$is_testtrial==1,]
df_allsubjs_traintrials <- df_allsubjs_trials[df_allsubjs_trials$state_group==0,]
df_allsubjs_traintrials$n_trials <- 12
df_allsubjs_testtrials$n_trials <- 16
```


```{r}

# ----- Metrics -----
log_loss <- function(y, p, eps = 1e-15) {
  p <- pmin(pmax(p, eps), 1 - eps)
  -mean(y * log(p) + (1 - y) * log(1 - p))
}

brier_score <- function(y, p) {
  mean((p - y)^2)
}

acc_at_0_5 <- function(y, p) {
  mean((p >= 0.5) == y)
}

# ----- Simple stratified K-fold (by binary response) -----
# ----- Stratified grouped K-fold for binary response -----
# Holds out entire participants while approximately balancing response rates.
make_stratified_folds <- function(y, group, K = 5, seed = 0) {
  set.seed(seed)

  y <- as.integer(y)
  group <- as.character(group)

  if (!all(y %in% c(0, 1))) {
    stop("y must be binary 0/1.")
  }

  group_levels <- unique(group)

  if (length(group_levels) < K) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "Number of groups (%d) is smaller than K (%d).",
      length(group_levels), K
    ))
  }

  # Summarize response distribution per participant
  group_summary <- data.frame(
    group = group_levels,
    n = as.integer(tabulate(match(group, group_levels))),
    n_pos = as.integer(tapply(y, group, sum)[group_levels])
  )

  group_summary$prop_pos <- group_summary$n_pos / group_summary$n

  # Randomize ties, then assign high/low-response participants across folds
  group_summary <- group_summary[sample(seq_len(nrow(group_summary))), ]
  group_summary <- group_summary[order(-group_summary$prop_pos), ]

  folds_groups <- vector("list", K)
  fold_n <- rep(0, K)
  fold_pos <- rep(0, K)

  global_prop <- mean(y)

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(group_summary))) {
    this_n <- group_summary$n[i]
    this_pos <- group_summary$n_pos[i]

    candidate_scores <- sapply(seq_len(K), function(k) {
      new_n <- fold_n[k] + this_n
      new_pos <- fold_pos[k] + this_pos
      new_prop <- new_pos / new_n

      # Balance response rate, with tiny penalty for oversized folds
      abs(new_prop - global_prop) + 0.001 * new_n
    })

    k_best <- which.min(candidate_scores)

    folds_groups[[k_best]] <- c(folds_groups[[k_best]], group_summary$group[i])
    fold_n[k_best] <- fold_n[k_best] + this_n
    fold_pos[k_best] <- fold_pos[k_best] + this_pos
  }

  # Return row indices, preserving old interface behavior
  folds <- lapply(folds_groups, function(gs) {
    which(group %in% gs)
  })

  folds
}

# ----- CV wrapper for GLM/GLMM -----
# ----- CV wrapper for GLM/GLMM: between-participant generalization -----
cv_glmm_kfold <- function(df, response, group, fixed_formula, random_spec = "",
                          K = 5, seed = 0,
                          optimizer = "bobyqa", nAGQ = 0) {

  # Determine whether we are running GLMM or GLM
  is_mixed <- !is.null(random_spec) && random_spec != ""

  # Build formula string
  fml_str <- if (is_mixed) {
    paste0(response, " ~ ", fixed_formula, " + ", random_spec)
  } else {
    paste0(response, " ~ ", fixed_formula)
  }

  fml <- as.formula(fml_str)

  # Sanity check for binary response
  uy <- sort(unique(df[[response]]))
  if (!all(uy %in% c(0, 1))) {
    stop(sprintf("%s must be 0/1", response))
  }

  # Ensure factor handling
  df[[group]] <- as.factor(df[[group]])

  if ("cond" %in% names(df) && !is.factor(df$cond)) {
    df$cond <- as.factor(df$cond)
  }

  # Fit on full data
  fit_full <- if (is_mixed) {
    suppressWarnings(
      glmer(
        fml,
        data = df,
        family = binomial(),
        control = glmerControl(optimizer = optimizer),
        nAGQ = nAGQ
      )
    )
  } else {
    glm(fml, data = df, family = binomial())
  }

  # Grouped stratified folds: all rows from a participant stay together
  folds <- make_stratified_folds(
    y = df[[response]],
    group = df[[group]],
    K = K,
    seed = seed
  )

  rows <- vector("list", K)

  for (k in seq_len(K)) {
    te_idx <- folds[[k]]
    tr_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(df)), te_idx)

    train <- droplevels(df[tr_idx, , drop = FALSE])
    test  <- df[te_idx, , drop = FALSE]

    # Align ordinary fixed-effect factor levels
    if ("cond" %in% names(train)) {
      test$cond <- factor(test$cond, levels = levels(train$cond))
    }

    # Preserve held-out subject IDs as new levels
    test[[group]] <- factor(
      as.character(test[[group]]),
      levels = union(
        levels(train[[group]]),
        unique(as.character(test[[group]]))
      )
    )

    # Fit and predict
    if (is_mixed) {
      fit_k <- suppressWarnings(
        glmer(
          fml,
          data = train,
          family = binomial(),
          control = glmerControl(optimizer = optimizer),
          nAGQ = nAGQ
        )
      )

      # Between-participant prediction:
      # held-out subjects have no estimated random effects, so use FE-only.
      p_test <- suppressWarnings(
        predict(
          fit_k,
          newdata = test,
          type = "response",
          re.form = NA,
          allow.new.levels = TRUE
        )
      )

      n_with_RE <- 0
      n_FE_only <- nrow(test)

    } else {
      fit_k <- glm(fml, data = train, family = binomial())

      p_test <- suppressWarnings(
        predict(fit_k, newdata = test, type = "response")
      )

      n_with_RE <- NA_integer_
      n_FE_only <- NA_integer_
    }

    y_test <- test[[response]]

    rows[[k]] <- data.frame(
      fold = k,
      n_train = nrow(train),
      n_test = nrow(test),
      n_train_subjects = dplyr::n_distinct(train[[group]]),
      n_test_subjects = dplyr::n_distinct(test[[group]]),
      n_with_RE = n_with_RE,
      n_FE_only = n_FE_only,
      mean_logloss = log_loss(y_test, p_test),
      brier = brier_score(y_test, p_test),
      acc = acc_at_0_5(y_test, p_test)
    )
  }

  out <- do.call(rbind, rows)

  avg <- colMeans(out[, c("mean_logloss", "brier", "acc")])

  list(
    cv_table = out,
    cv_avg = avg,
    fit_full = fit_full,
    model_type = ifelse(is_mixed, "GLMM", "GLM"),
    cv_type = "between-participant grouped K-fold"
  )
}

```


```{r}
# ----- Metric for regression -----
mse <- function(y, yhat) mean((y - yhat)^2)

# ----- Simple (non-stratified) K-fold for regression -----
# ----- Grouped random K-fold for regression -----
# Holds out entire participants.
make_random_folds <- function(group, K = 5, seed = 0) {
  set.seed(seed)

  group <- as.character(group)
  group_levels <- unique(group)

  if (length(group_levels) < K) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "Number of groups (%d) is smaller than K (%d).",
      length(group_levels), K
    ))
  }

  group_levels <- sample(group_levels)

  folds_groups <- split(
    group_levels,
    rep(seq_len(K), length.out = length(group_levels))
  )

  folds <- lapply(folds_groups, function(gs) {
    which(group %in% gs)
  })

  folds
}

# ----- CV wrapper for LME (test MSE; includes REs when available) -----
# ----- CV wrapper for LME: between-participant generalization -----
cv_lme_kfold <- function(df, response, group, fixed_formula, random_spec,
                         K = 5, seed = 0,
                         optimizer = "bobyqa") {
  # Basic checks
  if (!is.numeric(df[[response]])) {
    stop(sprintf("%s must be numeric for LME.", response))
  }

  df[[group]] <- as.factor(df[[group]])

  if ("cond" %in% names(df) && !is.factor(df$cond)) {
    df$cond <- as.factor(df$cond)
  }

  # Build model formula
  fml <- as.formula(
    paste0(response, " ~ ", fixed_formula, " + ", random_spec)
  )

  # Optional full-data fit
  fit_full <- suppressWarnings(
    lmer(
      fml,
      data = df,
      control = lmerControl(optimizer = optimizer),
      REML = FALSE
    )
  )

  # Grouped folds: all rows from a participant go into the same fold
  folds <- make_random_folds(
    group = df[[group]],
    K = K,
    seed = seed
  )

  rows <- vector("list", K)

  for (k in seq_len(K)) {
    te_idx <- folds[[k]]
    tr_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(df)), te_idx)

    train <- droplevels(df[tr_idx, , drop = FALSE])
    test  <- df[te_idx, , drop = FALSE]

    # Align ordinary fixed-effect factor levels
    if ("cond" %in% names(train)) {
      test$cond <- factor(test$cond, levels = levels(train$cond))
    }

    # Preserve held-out subject IDs as new levels
    test[[group]] <- factor(
      as.character(test[[group]]),
      levels = union(
        levels(train[[group]]),
        unique(as.character(test[[group]]))
      )
    )

    # Fit on training fold
    fit_k <- suppressWarnings(
      lmer(
        fml,
        data = train,
        control = lmerControl(optimizer = optimizer),
        REML = FALSE
      )
    )

    y_test <- test[[response]]

    # Between-participant prediction:
    # held-out participants have no random-effect estimates, so use FE-only.
    yhat <- suppressWarnings(
      predict(
        fit_k,
        newdata = test,
        re.form = NA,
        allow.new.levels = TRUE
      )
    )

    rows[[k]] <- data.frame(
      fold = k,
      n_train = nrow(train),
      n_test = nrow(test),
      n_train_subjects = dplyr::n_distinct(train[[group]]),
      n_test_subjects = dplyr::n_distinct(test[[group]]),
      n_with_RE = 0,
      n_FE_only = nrow(test),
      test_mse = mse(y_test, yhat)
    )
  }

  out <- do.call(rbind, rows)

  list(
    cv_table = out,
    cv_avg_mse = mean(out$test_mse),
    fit_full = fit_full,
    cv_type = "between-participant grouped K-fold"
  )
}
```



# Below is Conclusion I)

```{r}
response <- "use_cached_solution_path"   # 0/1
group    <- "subj_global"          # subject id column
seed <- 0
# Only trials where the default solution family is Einstellung.
df_temp = df_allsubjs_testtrials[df_allsubjs_testtrials$state_group>=2,]

# Compare which two conditions?
df_temp <- subset(df_temp, exp_cond %in% c("exp2color_0", "exp2visualsearch_1"))
df_temp$exp_cond <- droplevels(factor(df_temp$exp_cond))


fixed_formula0  <- "1"
random_spec0    <- "(1 | subj_global)"
res0b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_temp, response, group,
                      fixed_formula0, random_spec0, K = 5, seed = seed)
fixed_formula1  <- "test_condition"
random_spec1    <- "(1 | subj_global)"
res1b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_temp, response, group,
                      fixed_formula1, random_spec1, K = 5, seed = seed)
fixed_formula2 <- "solution_len_diff + trial_idx"
random_spec2   <- "(1+solution_len_diff + trial_idx || subj_global)"
res2b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_temp, response, group,
                      fixed_formula2, random_spec2, K = 5, seed = seed)

fixed_formula3 <- "test_condition + solution_len_diff + trial_idx"
random_spec3   <- "(1+solution_len_diff + trial_idx || subj_global)"
res3b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_temp, response, group,
                      fixed_formula3, random_spec3, K = 5, seed = seed)

fixed_formula4 <- "state_group + solution_len_diff + trial_idx"
random_spec4   <- "(1+state_group + solution_len_diff + trial_idx || subj_global)"
res4b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_temp, response, group,
                     fixed_formula4, random_spec4, K = 5, seed = seed)

fixed_formula5 <- "test_condition + state_group + solution_len_diff + trial_idx"
random_spec5   <- "(1+state_group + solution_len_diff + trial_idx || subj_global)"
res5b <- cv_glmm_kfold(df_temp, response, group,
                     fixed_formula5, random_spec5, K = 5, seed = seed)

```
```{r}
print(BIC(res0b$fit_full))
print(BIC(res1b$fit_full))
print(BIC(res2b$fit_full))
print(BIC(res3b$fit_full))
print(BIC(res4b$fit_full))
print(BIC(res5b$fit_full))
print(res0b$cv_avg)
print(res1b$cv_avg)
print(res2b$cv_avg)
print(res3b$cv_avg)
print(res4b$cv_avg)
print(res5b$cv_avg)
print(" ")
# summary(res0b$fit_full)
summary(res1b$fit_full)
# summary(res2b$fit_full)
summary(res3b$fit_full)
# summary(res4b$fit_full)
# summary(res5b$fit_full)

```
